meta.setting: Private mine office above French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in claim ownership and quiet pressure
meta.issue_title: Image: Claim Kings and Combine Men

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Mining communities, claims, and resource conflict.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Mining commerce, courthouse records, and old town material culture.

image_prompt.subject: well-dressed combine men studying claim maps in a private back room while a muddy miner waits outside frosted glass
image_prompt.scene: lamplit office; cigar haze, safe door, survey map with unreadable marks, polished boots against rough floorboards, and a ledger closed under one man's hand
image_prompt.composition: square interior with clean suits enclosed by dark wood and the waiting miner as a shadow beyond the door; claim papers form the center of gravity
image_prompt.camera: slightly high corner angle like a stealth overhear scene, with the threshold mud visible below
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: The room should look expensive enough to make the mud at the door feel like evidence.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine private office scene
render_traits: slightly simplified suited figures and office objects
modest texture resolution on paper, smoke, wood, wool, and iron
hard ambient occlusion under desk, safe, chairs, and door frame
warm lamplight trapped in a dark office
believable 1899 office furniture, clothing, and claim materials
quiet ownership pressure instead of villain poster
style_modifiers.palette: walnut brown, lamp amber, cigar gray, paper cream, suit black, mud umber, safe iron, and dull silver.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable maps, ledger entries, office signs, labels, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The combine men, claim maps, safe, office, waiting miner, and muddy threshold are readable.
Every visible prop belongs plausibly to an 1899 mine office.
The image shows ownership pressure and paper power without readable text.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
